dk.tohjuler.mcutils.items

Class ItemBuilder

  • java.lang.Object
    • dk.tohjuler.mcutils.items.ItemBuilder

  • public class ItemBuilder
extends Object

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      ItemBuilder(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack)
      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a ItemStack
      ItemBuilder(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item, @Nullable String headBase64)
      Used to clone an itembuilder
      ItemBuilder(org.bukkit.Material material)
      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a Material
      ItemBuilder(org.bukkit.Material material, int amount)
      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a Material and an amount

      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a Material, an amount and a durability

      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a head value
      ItemBuilder(@NotNull UUID uuid)
      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a UUID
      ItemBuilder(com.cryptomorin.xseries.XMaterial xMaterial)
      Create a new ItemBuilder, from a XMaterial

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      ItemBuilder addEnchantment(org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment enchantment, int level)
      Add an enchantment to the item

      Add itemFlags to the item
      ItemBuilder addLore(Collection<String> lines)
      Add a line to the lore
      ItemBuilder addLore(String... lore)
      Add a line to the lore
      ItemBuilder applyPlaceholder(org.bukkit.entity.Player p)
      Apply placeholders to the item This requires PlaceholderAPI to be installed Applies to: - Display name - Lore
      ItemBuilder applyType(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Transform the item to a different type This will keep the amount, display name, lore, enchantments, unstackable status and item flags
      org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack build()
      Build the item, why do I even explain this?
      dev.triumphteam.gui.guis.GuiItem buildAsGuiItem()
      Build the item, but as a GuiItem This is for Triumphteam's GUI
      dev.triumphteam.gui.guis.GuiItem buildAsGuiItem(dev.triumphteam.gui.components.GuiAction<org.bukkit.event.inventory.InventoryClickEvent> action)
      Build the item, but as a GuiItem This is for Triumphteam's GUI
      ItemBuilder clone() 
      ItemBuilder deserialize(String base64)
      Deserialize an item from a base64 itemstack Skull values DOES NOT WORK, it needs to be serialized from serialize() or ItemStackBase64.itemStackToBase64(ItemStack)
      static ItemBuilder fromBase64(String base64)
      Create a new ItemBuilder from a base64 itemstack Skull values DOES NOT WORK, it needs to be serialized from serialize() or ItemStackBase64.itemStackToBase64(ItemStack)
      static ItemBuilder fromString(String mat)
      Create a new ItemBuilder from a string.

      Get the item amount

      Get the item lore

      Get the tag of the item

      Hide the enchantments

      Modify the item, using a function Allows for more advanced modifications

      Remove a tag from the item

        public ItemBuilder applyPlaceholder(org.bukkit.entity.Player p)
        Apply placeholders to the item This requires PlaceholderAPI to be installed Applies to: - Display name - Lore

        the itembuilder

        Transform the item to a different type This will keep the amount, display name, lore, enchantments, unstackable status and item flags

        Returns:
        the itembuilder

        public ItemBuilder replaceAllFromGui(String regex,
                                     Function<String,String> func)

        func - the function to replace with

      • serialize

        public String serialize()
        Serialize the item to a base64 string
        Returns:
        the base64 string

      • deserialize

        public ItemBuilder deserialize(String base64)
        Deserialize an item from a base64 itemstack Skull values DOES NOT WORK, it needs to be serialized from serialize() or ItemStackBase64.itemStackToBase64(ItemStack)
        Parameters:
        base64 - the base64 string
        Returns:
        the itembuilder

      • fromBase64

        public static ItemBuilder fromBase64(String base64)
        Create a new ItemBuilder from a base64 itemstack Skull values DOES NOT WORK, it needs to be serialized from serialize() or ItemStackBase64.itemStackToBase64(ItemStack)
        Parameters:
        base64 - the base64 string
        Returns:
        the itembuilder

      • fromString

        public static ItemBuilder fromString(String mat)
        Create a new ItemBuilder from a string. Valid types: - Material name - Base64 skull value - UUID of a player

        Parameters:
        mat - the string
        Returns:
        the itembuilder
        Since:
        1.17.0
